summary: Admit one expression-prefix transactional <p/q> literal and normalize it to an exact BigInt pair.
requires: the lexical goal is expression prefix
both components are contiguous unsigned decimal magnitudes
normalization completes within the deterministic budget
preconditions: the candidate begins with < followed by a decimal digit
normalization: preserve raw CST magnitudes; reduce the mathematical BigInt pair by gcd, make the denominator positive, and canonicalize zero to 0/1
decision_procedure: probe the complete contiguous candidate without consuming on shape failure
validate unsigned decimal component spelling and absence of internal trivia
normalize within the declared resource budget
emit one RationalConst with the normalized Rational TypeId
termination_metric: finite candidate byte length plus strictly decreasing Euclidean gcd magnitudes
evaluation_order: goal -> transactional scan -> spelling -> denominator -> normalization -> HIR

source_name: Complex literal and closed numeric operation
summary: Admit attached floating i literals and the closed Float32/Float64 Complex numeric profile without implicit Rep conversion.
requires: Rep is exactly Float32 or Float64
imaginary literal marker is attached to one decimal floating token
operator pair is intrinsic or has one sealed Prelude +,-,* witness
no hidden mixed-Rep or integer conversion
preconditions: the literal or operator has one closed Complex owner
every component and operand TypeId is normalized
normalization: normalize bare Complex to Complex<Float64>; preserve exact Rep and IEEE component values including signed zero
decision_procedure: classify an attached i candidate before splitting a numeric suffix
require Float32 or Float64 and create a positive-zero real component
for +,-,* select the unique intrinsic-reserved pair or sealed direct-global Prelude witness
for / select the language-owned same-Rep intrinsic
reject remainder, total ordering, strong Eq/Keyable, mixed Rep, and implicit typed-integer adaptation
termination_metric: finite token and finite closed operator-domain matrix
evaluation_order: scan -> normalize Rep -> classify operator -> select intrinsic/witness -> HIR

summary: Resolve <T as Trait>::item through exactly one static conformance and preserve all identity and responsibility residue.
requires: the source uses an explicit <T as Trait>::item selector
one exact ConformanceId and TraitWitnessId are selected statically
the item kind agrees with the type or expression goal
all required identity and responsibility residue is complete
preconditions: T and Trait are normalized static identities
the associated selector is structurally complete
normalization: normalize T, Trait and substitution; retain RequirementId, ConformanceId, TraitWitnessId, ImplementationId and ResponsibilityId
decision_procedure: resolve only the explicitly named Trait domain
require one exact conformance/witness and one associated requirement identity
check item kind and the minimum associated let:: value profile where applicable
reject runtime lookup, provider/source order, fallback and companion-object synthesis
emit the complete HIR/API selection residue
termination_metric: finite explicitly named Trait requirement set
evaluation_order: normalize -> exact domain -> conformance/witness -> item kind -> residue -> HIR
